title: 船长电台007：爷爷奶奶都说哇了，MR赢了！
tweets:
An elderly man put on an MR headset at a Chongqing housing expo.

He stood still for 3 seconds.

Then said: "Wow."

Not at a tech conference. On a street. A real apartment he's buying, rendered 1:1 in front of his eyes before it's even built.

That moment, spatial AI won.

🧵 (1/5)

The tech behind it: 3DGS — 3D Gaussian Splatting.

Real apartment → scanned with professional gear → AI trains a real-time 3D model → rendered at 99% image fidelity inside an MR headset.

At 2m+ distance, it outperforms traditional VR camera shots.

This is industry-leading. Verified by benchmark. (2/5)

It took 3 versions to get here.

V1: Second-hand homes. Fatal flaw — model ready, house already sold.
V2: Cut quality to scale. Users said: "blurry, not worth it."
V3: DreamHome. Pivot to new homes + renovation. Same scan, many customers. Emotion-first design.

Right direction took time to find. (3/5)

The bigger question: why does this matter beyond real estate?

AI is mostly blind to physical space — how wide your door is, how light fills your room, how tall your ceiling feels.

Spatial intelligence is the bridge between AI and the real world.

Buying homes, renovating, community services — all of it needs this. (4/5)

Pico is reportedly launching a new device — better than Vision Pro, fraction of the weight.

When MR becomes wearable on the street, virtual and physical worlds truly merge.

AI needs real-world grounding. Humans need spatial imagination.

The convergence is coming.

🎙 Full ep: search「船长时空站」on Spotify / Apple Podcasts / 小宇宙 (5/5)
